task_completion.description: Create a View with a Block display for products
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Drupal Views configuration page showing a new view (e.g., 'Related Products') with a 'Block' display created.

task_completion.description: Configure the View to display exactly 3 items
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Views configuration page showing the Pager settings configured to display a specified number of items, set to exactly 3.

task_completion.description: Configure the View to sort products randomly
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Views configuration page showing 'Global: Random' listed under the Sort Criteria section.

task_completion.description: Configure a contextual filter to exclude the current product
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Views configuration page showing a Contextual Filter (e.g., Product ID) added, with settings indicating it provides a default value from the URL and the 'Exclude' option is checked.

task_completion.description: Place the block on product detail pages
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Drupal Block Layout page showing the 'Related Products' block placed in a theme region, or the block configuration page showing visibility restricted to product content types/pages.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Related Products' block appears on a product detail page with 3 random products, excluding the current one
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of a product detail page on the frontend showing the 'Related Products' block. It must contain exactly 3 products, and the product currently being viewed must not be among them.

integrity.description: Agent used the Drupal GUI to create the view and block
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Drupal admin web interface, not terminal commands (like drush) importing configuration.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Drupal application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine Drupal Commerce frontend and backend interfaces, not a text editor or mocked HTML pages.

integrity.description: The block is dynamic and not hardcoded
integrity.visual_evidence: The block should be a Views block, not a basic custom block with hardcoded HTML links and images of 3 products.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The environment is Drupal Commerce with 12 synthetically generated products that have real names (e.g., 'Sony WH-1000XM5', 'Apple MacBook Air M2', 'Samsung QLED TV'). The VLM should verify that the 'Related Products' block on the frontend displays exactly 3 of these products, and the current product being viewed is not in the list.
